focus: Evaluate deep-review output contracts and synthesis behavior
summary: Deep-review synthesis can hide shallow searches because verdict, severity counts, active finding registry, planning packet, and dashboard risk sections are strongly structured, while ruled-out/null-search evidence is optional, late, and unstructured. A less surface-level review needs a required search ledger or candidate coverage matrix that preserves rejected hypotheses and missed-class risks alongside active findings.

title: Synthesis verdict is severity-count based without a matching search-depth proof gate
implication: PASS or CONDITIONAL can be release-routing correct while still failing to prove that likely bug classes were actually searched.

title: Audit appendix is the only required home for ruled-out claims and sources reviewed
implication: Null-search evidence can collapse into prose at the end of the report instead of becoming a first-class searchable artifact.

title: Dashboard surfaces active risk but not search debt
implication: Operators can see P0/P1/P2 debt and blocked stops, but not missing null-search evidence or weak rejected-hypothesis coverage.

summary: The review state schema requires finding details but makes ruled-out evidence optional.
Convergence treats clean passes as zero novelty and makes dimension coverage the highest-weight signal, without requiring proof of candidate-class saturation.
Report examples vary: some preserve ruled-out dispositions, while others mostly summarize verdict, active findings, and sources reviewed.
Findings-first ordering is correct for human review, but deep-review needs additional negative-evidence persistence because its output drives convergence and release readiness.
When no severity or gate debt exists, the dashboard can report no active risk without revealing whether candidate bug classes were actually exhausted.

direction: Treat the problem as summary wording only
reason: The issue is structural, not cosmetic: required state/report/dashboard fields do not preserve null-search evidence with the same rigor as active findings.

direction: Rely on the existing Audit Appendix for null-search proof
reason: The appendix mentions ruled-out claims and sources reviewed but lacks required row schema, minimum coverage thresholds, or candidate-class saturation checks.
